Name: Barbell Bench Press
Description: Lie on a flat bench and press a loaded barbell from chest level to full arm extension. Compound movement for building upper body strength and chest mass.

Name: Dumbbell Bench Press
Description: Press two dumbbells from chest level to full arm extension while lying on a flat bench. Allows greater range of motion and independent arm movement to correct muscle imbalances.

Name: Incline Dumbbell Press
Description: Press dumbbells while lying on an inclined bench (30-45 degrees). Targets the upper portion of the chest and front shoulders.

Name: Chest Fly (Dumbbell)
Description: Lie on a bench with arms extended and arc dumbbells out to sides then back together. Isolates chest muscles with a stretch-focused movement.

Name: Push-Ups
Description: Classic bodyweight exercise performed face-down. Lower body to ground by bending elbows then push back up. Develops chest, shoulders, and triceps.

Name: Cable Chest Fly
Description: Using cable machine, bring handles together in front of chest from extended position. Provides constant tension throughout the movement for chest isolation.

Name: Pull-Ups
Description: Hang from a bar with overhand grip and pull body up until chin clears bar. Compound exercise for developing back width and arm strength.

Name: Lat Pulldown
Description: Seated cable exercise where you pull a bar down to upper chest level. Builds back width and strength using a controlled vertical pulling motion.

Name: Barbell Row
Description: Bend forward at hips and row a barbell to lower chest/upper abdomen. Fundamental compound movement for building back thickness and mid-back strength.

Name: Dumbbell Row
Description: Single or double-arm rowing movement with dumbbells. Allows unilateral training and increased range of motion for back development.

Name: Seated Cable Row
Description: Pull cable handle to torso while seated with legs extended. Targets mid-back and develops thickness in the latissimus dorsi.

Name: Face Pull
Description: Pull rope attachment toward face with high elbows using cable machine. Essential for rear deltoid and upper back health.

Name: T-Bar Row
Description: Row a barbell loaded on one end while straddling it. Heavy compound movement for building back thickness and overall mass.

Name: Inverted Row
Description: Bodyweight row performed under a fixed bar or with suspension straps. Horizontal pulling exercise that develops back and arm strength.

Name: Overhead Barbell Press
Description: Press barbell from shoulder height to overhead lockout while standing or seated. Primary compound movement for shoulder mass and strength development.

Name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
Description: Press dumbbells overhead from shoulder position. Allows natural movement path and individual arm development.

Name: Lateral Raises
Description: Raise dumbbells or cables out to sides with slight bend in elbows. Isolates middle deltoid for shoulder width development.

Name: Front Raises
Description: Raise weight from thighs to shoulder height in front of body. Targets anterior deltoids and improves shoulder definition.

Name: Rear Delt Fly
Description: Bent-over fly movement targeting rear deltoids. Critical for balanced shoulder development and posture.

Name: Arnold Press
Description: Rotating dumbbell press that starts with palms facing body and rotates outward. Works all three deltoid heads through full range of motion.

Name: Barbell Curl
Description: Standing bicep curl with barbell using underhand grip. Fundamental exercise for building bicep mass and arm thickness.

Name: Dumbbell Curl
Description: Curl dumbbells with alternating or simultaneous arm movement. Allows supination and natural wrist position for bicep development.

Name: Hammer Curl
Description: Curl dumbbells with neutral (thumbs-up) grip. Targets biceps and forearm brachialis for arm thickness.

Name: Preacher Curl
Description: Bicep curl performed on preacher bench with arms braced. Isolates biceps by preventing body momentum.

Name: Tricep Dips
Description: Lower and raise body between parallel bars using triceps. Bodyweight exercise for tricep mass and strength.

Name: Skull Crushers
Description: Lying tricep extension lowering weight toward forehead. Isolation movement for long head of triceps.

Name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
Description: Push cable attachment down to full arm extension. Tricep isolation exercise with constant tension.

Name: Overhead Tricep Extension
Description: Extend weight overhead from behind head. Stretches and works the long head of triceps effectively.

Name: Barbell Squat
Description: Lower into squat position with barbell on upper back then stand. Fundamental compound leg exercise for overall lower body development.

Name: Goblet Squat
Description: Squat while holding dumbbell or kettlebell at chest level. Effective for learning proper squat mechanics and quad development.

Name: Leg Press
Description: Push weighted platform away using legs while seated or reclined. Machine-based leg exercise allowing heavy loading with lower back support.

Name: Walking Lunges
Description: Step forward into lunge position alternating legs while moving forward. Builds leg strength, balance, and athletic movement patterns.

Name: Bulgarian Split Squat
Description: Single-leg squat with rear foot elevated on bench. Challenging unilateral exercise for quad, glute, and stability development.

Name: Step-Ups
Description: Step onto elevated platform alternating legs. Functional leg exercise that builds strength and improves coordination.

Name: Jump Squats
Description: Explosive squat variation where you jump at the top of movement. Develops lower body power and athletic performance.

Name: Wall Sit
Description: Hold squat position with back against wall for time. Isometric exercise building leg endurance and mental toughness.

Name: Romanian Deadlift
Description: Hip hinge movement lowering weight along legs while maintaining straight back. Targets hamstrings and glutes with emphasis on stretch.

Name: Conventional Deadlift
Description: Lift barbell from floor to standing position using full-body mechanics. Powerful compound movement for total body strength and posterior chain development.

Name: Hip Thrust
Description: Drive hips upward while upper back is braced on bench. Highly effective exercise for isolated glute development and strength.

Name: Hamstring Curl
Description: Curl weight toward glutes using machine. Direct hamstring isolation for building strength and size.

Name: Glute Bridge
Description: Lift hips off ground while lying on back with knees bent. Fundamental glute activation and strengthening exercise.

Name: Kettlebell Swing
Description: Explosive hip hinge swinging kettlebell from between legs to chest height. Develops posterior chain power and cardiovascular endurance.

Name: Plank
Description: Hold rigid body position on forearms and toes. Core stability exercise that builds endurance in abdominal and back muscles.

Name: Side Plank
Description: Hold body sideways on one forearm and side of foot. Targets obliques and lateral core stability.

Name: Bicycle Crunch
Description: Alternating elbow-to-knee crunches with cycling leg motion. Dynamic ab exercise working entire core with rotation.

Name: Leg Raises
Description: Raise legs from hanging or lying position. Lower ab focused exercise that also engages hip flexors.

Name: Russian Twist
Description: Rotate torso side to side while holding weight in seated position. Trains rotational core strength and obliques.

Name: Hanging Leg Raise
Description: Raise legs to horizontal or higher while hanging from bar. Challenging core exercise emphasizing lower abs and hip flexors.

Name: Ab Wheel Rollout
Description: Roll ab wheel forward to extended position then return. Demanding anti-extension core exercise for experienced athletes.

Name: Mountain Climbers
Description: Drive knees toward chest alternately in plank position. Dynamic cardio and core exercise that elevates heart rate.

Name: Burpees
Description: Full-body movement combining squat, plank, push-up, and jump. High-intensity conditioning exercise that builds endurance and burns calories.

Name: Jump Rope
Description: Traditional rope jumping for time or repetitions. Excellent cardiovascular exercise that improves coordination and conditioning.

Name: High Knees
Description: Running in place driving knees to hip height. Cardio exercise that develops leg speed and cardiovascular endurance.

Name: Sprint Intervals
Description: Alternate between maximum effort sprints and recovery periods. High-intensity training for speed, power, and metabolic conditioning.

Name: Rowing Machine
Description: Full-body cardio using rowing ergometer. Develops cardiovascular fitness while engaging legs, back, and arms.

Name: Treadmill Run
Description: Running or walking on motorized treadmill. Versatile cardio option allowing precise pace and incline control.

Name: Cycling
Description: Stationary or outdoor cycling for cardiovascular training. Low-impact cardio excellent for endurance and leg conditioning.

Name: Stair Climber
Description: Continuous stair-climbing motion on machine. Challenging cardio that targets legs and elevates heart rate effectively.

Name: Battle Ropes
Description: Create waves with heavy ropes in various patterns. High-intensity conditioning exercise building power endurance and grip strength.

Name: Medicine Ball Slams
Description: Forcefully slam weighted ball to ground and catch on rebound. Explosive full-body exercise for power development and stress relief.

Name: Farmer's Carry
Description: Walk while carrying heavy weights at sides. Functional exercise building grip strength, core stability, and total body endurance.

Name: Sled Push
Description: Push weighted sled across ground. Lower body and conditioning exercise that develops power without eccentric loading.

Name: Box Jumps
Description: Jump onto elevated platform from standing position. Plyometric exercise for developing explosive lower body power.

Name: Agility Ladder Drills
Description: Quick footwork patterns through ladder on ground. Improves foot speed, coordination, and agility for sports performance.

Name: Tire Flips
Description: Flip large tire end-over-end. Total body power exercise combining hip drive, pulling, and pushing movements.

Name: Dynamic Stretch Circuit
Description: Series of active stretching movements performed in sequence. Prepares body for training by increasing blood flow and range of motion.

Name: Foam Rolling
Description: Self-massage technique using foam roller on muscles. Recovery method that reduces muscle tension and improves tissue quality.

Name: Hip Flexor Stretch
Description: Stretch front of hip in lunge or kneeling position. Addresses tightness from prolonged sitting and improves hip mobility.

Name: Hamstring Stretch
Description: Stretch back of leg while seated, standing, or lying down. Improves flexibility and reduces lower back tension.

Name: Shoulder Mobility Band Work
Description: Use resistance band for shoulder mobility exercises. Improves range of motion and prepares shoulders for training.

Name: Pec Deck
Description: Bring arms together in front of chest using machine. Isolated chest exercise with fixed movement path for focused muscle contraction.

Name: Assisted Pull-Up
Description: Vertical pulling exercise performed with machine or band assistance. Allows development of strength needed for bodyweight variations.

Name: Smith Machine Squat
Description: Squat with barbell on fixed vertical track. Provides stability for learning movement pattern or training with specific limitations.

Name: Leg Extension
Description: Extend legs against resistance while seated on machine. Isolation exercise specifically targeting quadriceps muscles.

Name: Calf Raises
Description: Rise onto toes against resistance from standing or seated position. Develops calf muscles for lower leg strength and aesthetics.

Name: Cable Woodchopper
Description: Diagonal chopping motion using cable machine with rotation. Rotational core exercise that strengthens obliques and improves athletic movement.

Name: Power Clean
Description: Explosively lift barbell from floor to shoulder position in one fluid motion. Olympic lifting variation that builds total body power and athleticism.

Name: Snatch
Description: Lift barbell from floor to overhead in one explosive motion. Technical Olympic lift requiring speed, power, and mobility.

Name: Push Press
Description: Use leg drive to assist pressing weight overhead. Combines lower body power with shoulder pressing for increased loading capacity.

Name: Depth Jumps
Description: Step off box and immediately jump upon landing with minimal ground contact. Advanced plyometric for developing reactive strength and explosive power.
